title: Abstract virtual class must redeclare inherited pure virtual method
body:
Verilator requires an abstract derived virtual class to redeclare a pure virtual method inherited from its abstract base class.

#### Code:
```systemverilog
virtual class Base;
  pure virtual function void f0();
endclass

virtual class Child extends Base;
  pure virtual function void f1();
endclass
```

#### Actual behavior:
Verilator reports that Child must implement `f0()`:
```
$ verilator --sv --lint-only repro.sv
%Error: repro.sv:5:9: Class 'Child' extends 'Base' but is missing implementation for 'f0' (IEEE 1800-2023 8.26)
    5 | virtual class Child extends Base;
      |         ^~~~~
        repro.sv:2:30: ... Location of interface class's function
    2 |   pure virtual function void f0();
      |                              ^~
        ... See the manual at https://verilator.org/verilator_doc.html?v=5.047 for more assistance.
%Error: Exiting due to 1 error(s)
```

#### Expected:
Child is abstract, so `f0()` should remain inherited as pure virtual.

#### Posible fix:
```diff
diff --git a/src/V3LinkDot.cpp b/src/V3LinkDot.cpp
index c9174bcc..5054de9d 100644
--- a/src/V3LinkDot.cpp
+++ b/src/V3LinkDot.cpp
@@ -3406,7 +3406,7 @@ class LinkDotResolveVisitor final : public VNVisitor {
                     if (!baseFuncp || !baseFuncp->pureVirtual()) continue;
                     const bool existsInDerived = foundp && !foundp->imported();
                     if (m_statep->forPrimary() && !existsInDerived
-                        && !derivedClassp->isInterfaceClass()) {
+                        && !derivedClassp->isInterfaceClass() && !derivedClassp->isVirtual()) {
                         derivedClassp->v3error(
                             "Class " << derivedClassp->prettyNameQ() << impOrExtends
                                      << baseClassp->prettyNameQ()
```

I'll prepare a pull request, if you don't mind.

#### Version
```sh
$ verilator --version
Verilator 5.047 devel rev v5.046-200-gbe6780e44 (mod)
```
